brisk.name: Brisk Teaching
brisk.studentNote: Staff use only — not approved for direct student use

tips.title: Turn Any Unit Into Self-Grading Flashcards and Quizzes with NotebookLM
tips.teaser: Progress now saves between sessions, so students can pick up a review deck right where they left off.

video.title: NotebookLM: flashcard and quizzes #hourofai #edtech
video.channel: Not independently confirmed — this is a YouTube Short, which guarantees a runtime under 60 seconds regardless of channel
video.description: A quick vertical demo of generating a flashcard deck and quiz directly from uploaded sources in NotebookLM's Studio panel — every card and question grounded only in the material you gave it.

tips.whyItMatters: Point NotebookLM at your unit's readings and it builds a flashcard deck and a quiz in seconds, grounded only in what you uploaded — no generic test-prep filler. A recent update means students can now mark cards "Got it" or "Missed it," shuffle the deck, and pick up their progress in a later session instead of starting over. It's a fast way to hand students a review tool that can't drift from your actual material.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff and student use with your guhsd.net account. Keep student-identifying records out of any uploaded source.

tips.title: Let Students Interrupt the Podcast: NotebookLM's Interactive Mode
tips.teaser: Students can now raise their hand mid-podcast and ask the AI hosts a follow-up question, live.

video.title: The new interactive mode in NotebookLM is blowing my mind as a teacher
video.channel: Not independently confirmed — this is a YouTube Short, which guarantees a runtime under 60 seconds regardless of channel
video.description: A teacher's quick reaction demo to NotebookLM's Interactive Mode: while a two-host Audio Overview is playing, a student can hit Join and ask a spoken question — the hosts answer from the uploaded sources, then pick the discussion back up.

tips.whyItMatters: The Audio Overview podcast feature was already a hit for auditory review — Interactive Mode takes it further by letting a student jump in with a question mid-conversation instead of just listening passively. The hosts answer using only the sources you uploaded, then resume where they left off, which turns a one-way summary into something closer to a Socratic study session.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff and student use with your guhsd.net account. Interactive Mode's spoken Q&A is English-only for now; the audio overview itself is available in 80+ languages.

tips.title: Turn Gemini Into a Personal Tutor with Study Notebooks
tips.teaser: Upload your unit materials and Gemini builds a diagnostic quiz, bite-sized lessons, and a live progress dashboard — automatically.

video.title: New Study Notebooks in Google Gemini
video.channel: Not independently confirmed — this is a YouTube Short, which guarantees a runtime under 60 seconds regardless of channel
video.description: A quick vertical demo of Gemini's new study notebooks feature: upload course materials, take a short diagnostic quiz, and watch Gemini build a personalized set of bite-sized lessons around exactly what needs the most work.

tips.whyItMatters: Study notebooks turn Gemini from a one-off chatbot into an adaptive study partner: a student uploads their own notes or a unit packet, takes a short diagnostic quiz, and gets a lesson plan built around their actual gaps — not a generic review sheet. Progress updates automatically as they complete follow-up quizzes, so both of you can see what's mastered and what still needs work. This feature is still expanding to school-issued accounts for students under 18, so confirm it's visible for your class before assigning it.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff and student use with your guhsd.net account. Study notebooks are still rolling out to under-18 school accounts — check availability before assigning.

tips.title: Talk to Every Family in Their Own Language with Brisk
tips.teaser: Translate a note home, a newsletter, or feedback into a family's home language in one click.

video.title: Brisk Bites 29 — Translate Text Tool
video.description: A short Brisk Bites walkthrough of the Translate Text tool — select any text in a Google Doc, Slide, or webpage and get an instant translation into a family's home language, without leaving the page.

tips.whyItMatters: A permission slip, a progress note, or written feedback lands very differently for a family who doesn't read English fluently. Brisk's translate tool turns any selected text into another language in seconds, right where you're already working — no separate app, no copy-pasting into a translator and losing formatting.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff use. Brisk is not currently approved for direct student use in GUHSD.

tips.title: Turn Dense Reading Into a Podcast Your Students Will Actually Listen To
tips.teaser: Upload a unit's readings and get a two-host podcast discussion of the material — auditory review, on autopilot.

video.title: NotebookLM audio overviews! #notebookLM
video.channel: Not independently confirmed — this is a YouTube Short, which guarantees a runtime under 60 seconds regardless of channel
video.description: A quick vertical demo of NotebookLM's Audio Overview feature: upload your sources and NotebookLM generates a podcast-style conversation between two AI hosts summarizing the material, grounded only in what you gave it.

tips.whyItMatters: Some students retain material better by listening than reading — this is especially true for multilingual learners and students who find dense text overwhelming. Upload your unit's readings and NotebookLM generates a podcast-style discussion of the content, which can now be generated in 80+ languages. Great for flipped-classroom pre-reading, a review before a test, or an alternate way into a tough text.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff and student use with your guhsd.net account. Keep student-identifying records out of any uploaded source.

tips.title: Turn Any YouTube Video Into an Instant Quiz with Brisk
tips.teaser: Point Brisk at a video or article and get a ready-to-grade quiz in under a minute.

video.title: Brisk Bites 3 — AI Quiz Generator
video.description: A bite-sized Brisk Teaching PD video walking through the AI Quiz Generator — point the Brisk extension at a webpage, PDF, or YouTube video and generate a standards-aligned quiz with an answer key already built in.

tips.whyItMatters: Skip writing a comprehension check from scratch. Point Brisk at the video or article you already assigned and get a ready-to-grade quiz — multiple choice, short answer, or open-ended, delivered to a Google Form or Doc — in under a minute. Handy for flipped-classroom check-ins, exit tickets, or catch-up work for an absent student.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff use. Brisk is not currently approved for direct student use in GUHSD — assign the generated quiz through your own Google Form or Doc.

tips.title: Differentiate Any Reading in Three Prompts, Not Three Prep Periods
tips.teaser: One passage in, three reading levels out — in the time it took to rewrite one paragraph.

video.title: Using Gemini for Google Workspace as an Educator
video.description: A Google for Education walkthrough of how teachers use Gemini inside the Workspace tools they already have open — drafting materials, adjusting reading levels, and building differentiated versions of an assignment without leaving Docs or Slides.

tips.whyItMatters: Every class has a range of readers. Ask Gemini to take one passage and generate a below-level, on-level, and extension version — same content, three entry points — in the time it used to take to rewrite one paragraph by hand. Students get material they can actually access; you get your prep period back.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff and student use with your guhsd.net account. Keep prompts free of student names or identifying details.

tips.title: Feedback That Lands: Skip the Generic 'Good Job' with Brisk
tips.teaser: Draft specific, rubric-aligned comments in seconds instead of retyping the same note all period.

video.title: How to Use Brisk: Brisk Teaching Overview
video.description: A guided tour of the Brisk Chrome extension inside a student's Google Doc — choosing a feedback style (Glows & Grows, rubric-based, or next-steps) and generating specific, actionable comments in place of a rubric score alone.

tips.whyItMatters: Targeted feedback is one of the highest-leverage moves a teacher can make — and one of the most time-consuming to do well for 150 students. Brisk drafts specific, rubric-aligned comments in seconds so you can spend your energy on the students who need a real conversation, not retyping the same note for the fifth time.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff use. Brisk is not currently approved for direct student use in GUHSD — the teacher remains the one giving feedback.

tips.title: Turn Any Unit Packet into a Study Partner with NotebookLM
tips.teaser: A study partner grounded only in the sources you upload — it can't wander off-topic.

video.title: An Introduction to Google NotebookLM
video.description: A short demonstration of uploading course materials into NotebookLM and using its source-grounded chat to generate summaries and answer questions — with every answer citing back to the exact page it came from.

tips.whyItMatters: Upload your unit's readings, slides, and notes, and NotebookLM only answers from what you gave it — no outside internet content, no invented facts. Students can ask it to quiz them or explain a confusing paragraph, and every answer links back to the source. It's a study partner that can't wander off-topic.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff and student use with your guhsd.net account. Keep student-identifying records (IEPs, grades, behavior notes) out of any uploaded source.
